title: Deployment Environments, OIDC, Workload Identity, and Cloud Authority · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Cloud Least Privilege Service Account
summary: A deployment uses a long-lived service-account key in repository secrets, trusts any branch subject, grants project-wide admin, bypasses environment review, and leaves credentials reusable after the job. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci cloud least privilege service account increment in environment and federated identity policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate build, registry, deploy, runtime, traffic, and rollback identities and grant resource-scoped roles for exact operations.
Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example deployment stores a long-lived cloud key, requests broad id-token and repository permissions, and trusts ambiguous OIDC claims. Constrain environment, audience, subject, workflow, revision, service account, approval, and audit evidence. Competency probe: Separate build, registry, deploy, runtime, traffic, and rollback identities and grant resource-scoped roles for exact operations. A defensible solution applies Ci Cloud Least Privilege Service Account, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to environment and federated identity policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-cd-environments-oidc-cloud-auth-2
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-environments-oidc-cloud-auth-2
    artifact: federated-deployment.yaml
    decision: worked cd environments oidc cloud auth 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      environment: production
      reviewers: operations-and-security
      branch-policy: protected-main
      permissions: contents-read-id-token-write
      oidc-issuer: token.actions.githubusercontent.com
      oidc-audience: google-wif-provider
      subject: immutable-repository-workflow-environment
      google-auth-action: 3
      service-account: cloud-run-deployer
      roles: resource-scoped
      credential-lifetime: job-bounded
    verify: inspect environment history OIDC claims provider condition service-account impersonation IAM audit and credential cleanup
    failure: Reject long-lived keys, repository-name-only trust, global admin, approval-free environments, or short lifetime as sufficient control.
    repair: Constrain environment and claims, federate identity, separate service accounts, scope roles, audit exchange, expire credentials, and rehearse revocation.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Cloud Run Admin alone is the complete and least-privilege deployment permission set. This fails the Ci Cloud Least Privilege Service Account contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
